name: T.m.'s Gingerbread Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Jo Anne Merrill
Preparation Time: 0:30
1. Sift flour, baking soda, salt, ginger, and nutmeg together.
2. In a large bowl of electric mixer, beat margarine, sugar, and egg at medium speed until light and fluffy. Mix in molasses at low speed until smooth.
3. Gradually add the sifted dry ingredients, beating until well-combined and smooth. Form the dough into a ball, wrap tightly, and refrigerate for at least 8 hours or overnight.
4. Divide the dough into 4 parts. Work with one part at a time, refrigerating the remaining portions.
5. On a lightly floured surface, roll each portion of dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch. Use gingerbread cutters or cookie cutters about 2-1/2 inches around to cut out cookies. Place cookies on lightly greased cookie sheets using a spatula. Gently press small pieces of dried fruit onto the dough for decoration.
6. Bake in a preheated oven at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 10-12 minutes or until browned, but do not overcook. Remove cookies to a wire rack to cool. Continue with the remaining dough portions, greasing the cookie sheets as needed.
Yield: About 30-36 cookies.
